heading: Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund
label: 9509 Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund

Report on Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–295, title III, § 322(a)116 Stat. 2103Nov. 25, 2002“The report regarding the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund required by the Conference Report (House Report 101–892) accompanying the Department of Transportation and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1991, [] as that requirement was amended by section 1122 of the Federal Reports Elimination and Sunset Act of 1995 () [see below], shall no longer be submitted to the Congress.”Pub. L. 101–516Public Law 104–66, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–66, title I, § 1122(a)109 Stat. 724Dec. 21, 1995“The quarterly report regarding the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund required to be submitted to the House and Senate Committees on Appropriations under House Report 101–892, accompanying the appropriations for the Coast Guard in the Department of Transportation and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1991 [], shall be submitted not later than 30 days after the end of the fiscal year in which this Act is enacted and annually thereafter.”Pub. L. 101–516[House Report 101–892, 101st Congress, 2d Session, provided that: “The conferees direct the Coast Guard to submit quarterly reports to the House and Senate Committee on Appropriations detailing and summarizing all transfers to and expenditures from the oil spill liability trust fund. Each report shall account for each transfer to and expenditure from the fund as authorized by Section 9509 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended, and Sections 5003 and 5004 of the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 () [, 2734]. The report shall also show amounts collectable under Section 9509(b)(2), (3), and (8) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986. For those authorized expenditures subject to limitations, the report shall so indicate. The Coast Guard shall confer with the House and Senate Committees on Appropriations as to the format for these reports.”]Public Law 101–38033 U.S.C. 2733

Deepwater Port Liability Fund, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–380, title II, § 2003(b)104 Stat. 507Aug. 18, 1990“Any amounts remaining in the Deepwater Port Liability Fund established under section 18(f) of the Deepwater Port Act of 1974 (33 U.S.C. [former] 1517(f)) shall be deposited in the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund established under section 9509 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (). The Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund shall assume all liability incurred by the Deepwater Port Liability Fund.”26 U.S.C. 9509

Offshore Oil Pollution Compensation Fund, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–380, title II, § 2004104 Stat. 507Aug. 18, 1990“Title III of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act Amendments of 1978 (4) is repealed. Any amounts remaining in the Offshore Oil Pollution Compensation Fund established under section 302 of that title () shall be deposited in the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund established under section 9509 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (). The Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund shall assume all liability incurred by the Offshore Oil Pollution Compensation Fund.”43 U.S.C. 1811–18243 U.S.C. 181226 U.S.C. 9509

L. 99–499section 9601 of Title 42“(A)subsection (a) of this section shall be applied by substituting ‘section 9508’ for ‘section 9506’,“(B)section 9507 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986], as added by this section, is hereby redesignated as section 9509 of such Code, and“(C)in lieu of the amendment made by subsection (b), the table of sections for subchapter A of chapter 98 of such Code is amended by adding after the item relating to section 9508 the following new item:“ ‘Sec. 9509. Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund.’ ”

children.heading: Creation of Trust Fund
children.label: a Creation of Trust Fund
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a trust fund to be known as the “Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund”, consisting of such amounts as may be appropriated or credited to such Trust Fund as provided in this section or section 9602(b).
taxes received in the Treasury under section 4611 (relating to environmental tax on petroleum) to the extent attributable to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund financing rate under section 4611(c),
amounts recovered under the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 for damages to natural resources which are required to be deposited in the Fund under section 1006(f) of such Act,
amounts recovered by such Trust Fund under section 1015 of such Act,
amounts required to be transferred by such Act from the revolving fund established under section 311(k) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act,
amounts required to be transferred by the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 from the Deepwater Port Liability Fund established under section 18(f) of the Deepwater Port Act of 1974,

amounts required to be transferred by the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 from the Offshore Oil Pollution Compensation Fund established under section 302 of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act Amendments of 1978,
amounts required to be transferred by the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 from the Trans-Alaska Pipeline Liability Fund established under section 204 of the Trans-Alaska Pipeline Authorization Act, and

any penalty paid pursuant to section 311 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act, section 309(c) of such Act (as a result of violations of such section 311), the Deepwater Port Act of 1974, or section 207 of the Trans-Alaska Pipeline Authorization Act.

children.label: A
children.text: for the payment of removal costs and other costs, expenses, claims, and damages referred to in section 1012 of such Act,

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out sections 5 and 7 of the Intervention on the High Seas Act relating to oil pollution or the substantial threat of oil pollution,

children.label: C
children.text: for the payment of liabilities incurred by the revolving fund established by section 311(k) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act,

children.label: D
children.text: to carry out subsections (b), (c), (d), (j), and () of section 311 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act with respect to prevention, removal, and enforcement related to oil discharges (as defined in such section),l

children.label: E
children.text: for the payment of liabilities incurred by the Deepwater Port Liability Fund, and

children.label: F
children.text: for the payment of liabilities incurred by the Offshore Oil Pollution Compensation Fund.

children.label: i
children.text: any single incident shall not exceed $1,500,000,000, and

children.label: ii
children.text: natural resource damage assessments and claims in connection with any single incident shall not exceed $750,000,000.

children.heading: $30,000,000 minimum balance
children.label: B $30,000,000 minimum balance
children.text: Except in the case of payments of removal costs, a payment may be made from such Trust Fund only if the amount in such Trust Fund after such payment will not be less than $30,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund, as repayable advances, such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of such Trust Fund.

children.heading: Limitation on amount outstanding
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount outstanding
children.text: The maximum aggregate amount of repayable advances to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund which is outstanding at any one time shall not exceed $1,000,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Advances made to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund shall be repaid, and interest on such advances shall be paid, to the general fund of the Treasury when the Secretary determines that moneys are available for such purposes in such Fund.

children.heading: Final repayment
children.label: B Final repayment
children.text: No advance shall be made to the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund after , and all advances to such Fund shall be repaid on or before such date.December 31, 1994

children.label: i
children.text: at a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury (as of the close of the calendar month preceding the month in which the advance is made) to be equal to the current average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States with remaining periods to maturity comparable to the anticipated period during which the advance will be outstanding, and

children.label: ii
children.text: compounded annually.

children.heading: Liability of the United States limited to amount in Trust Fund
children.label: e Liability of the United States limited to amount in Trust Fund

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Any claim filed against the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund may be paid only out of such Trust Fund.

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: 2 Coordination with other provisions
children.text: Nothing in the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 (or in any amendment made by such Act) shall authorize the payment by the United States Government of any amount with respect to any such claim out of any source other than the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund.

children.heading: Order in which unpaid claims are to be paid
children.label: 3 Order in which unpaid claims are to be paid
children.text: If at any time the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund has insufficient funds (or is unable by reason of subsection (c)(2)) to pay all of the claims out of such Trust Fund at such time, such claims shall, to the extent permitted under paragraph (1) and such subsection, be paid in full in the order in which they were finally determined.

children.heading: References to Oil Pollution Act of 1990
children.label: f References to Oil Pollution Act of 1990
children.text: Any reference in this section to the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 or any other Act referred to in a subparagraph of subsection (c)(1) shall be treated as a reference to such Act as in effect on the date of the enactment of this subsection.
